sub_problems: How does an agent at retrieval-trigger time distinguish self-generated text (its own prior tokens, scratchpad, internal monologue) from externally-supplied text (user input, tool output) when both flow through the same context window?

What is the minimal token-local signature that can be appended to external input by the runtime — a marker the model is trained to require for retrieval-action firing — without disrupting the model's natural ability to read the text?
How does the discriminator handle adversarial inputs that try to forge the signature, and what is the failure mode under a forging attack?
How does the system manage signatures across multi-step agent traces where external input gets processed, transformed, and re-emitted as internal scratchpad?
